title: Probability, Combinatorics, and Binomial Distribution
description: Python code snippets for probability concepts aligned with lab exercises on factorials, permutations, combinations, enumeration, and binomial distributions for infection modeling.

snippets.title: Library Imports
snippets.description: Standard imports for probability and combinatorics work
snippets.code:
%matplotlib inline
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from itertools import permutations
from itertools import combinations
from scipy.stats import binom
import math

snippets.title: Multiplication Principle
snippets.description: Apply the multiplication principle for counting arrangements
snippets.code:
# Example: Routes from Town A to C via B
routes_A_to_B = 4
routes_B_to_C = 2
total_routes = routes_A_to_B * routes_B_to_C
print(f"Total routes: {total_routes}")

# Arranging 5 letters: 5 × 4 × 3 × 2 × 1 = 5!
arrangements = 5 * 4 * 3 * 2 * 1
print(f"Ways to arrange 5 letters: {arrangements}")
snippets.expected_output:
Total routes: 8
Ways to arrange 5 letters: 120

snippets.title: Factorial Calculations
snippets.description: Use math.factorial() for factorial computations
snippets.code:
import math

# Basic factorials
print(f"5! = {math.factorial(5)}")
print(f"6! = {math.factorial(6)}")
print(f"0! = {math.factorial(0)}")

# Simplifying expressions
# 12!/10! = 12 × 11 = 132
result1 = math.factorial(12) / math.factorial(10)
print(f"12!/10! = {result1}")

# 6!/(2!4!) = 15
result2 = math.factorial(6) / (math.factorial(2) * math.factorial(4))
print(f"6!/(2!4!) = {result2}")

snippets.title: Permutation Formula
snippets.description: Calculate permutations using the formula P(n,p) = n!/(n-p)!
snippets.code:
import math

# P(n,p) = n!/(n-p)!
n = 3
p = 2
permutations_count = math.factorial(n) / math.factorial(n - p)
print(f"P({n},{p}) = {permutations_count}")

# P(5,3)
n, p = 5, 3
result = math.factorial(n) / math.factorial(n - p)
print(f"P({n},{p}) = {result}")

snippets.title: Listing Permutations with itertools
snippets.description: Use itertools.permutations to enumerate all ordered arrangements
snippets.code:
from itertools import permutations

# Permutations of 3 things taken 3 at a time
P33 = permutations(['A', 'B', 'C'], 3)
print("Permutations of A,B,C taken 3 at a time:")
for i in list(P33):
    print(i)

print()

# Permutations of 3 things taken 2 at a time
P32 = permutations(['A', 'B', 'C'], 2)
print("Permutations of A,B,C taken 2 at a time:")
for i in list(P32):
    print(i)
snippets.expected_output:
Permutations of A,B,C taken 3 at a time:
('A', 'B', 'C')
('A', 'C', 'B')
('B', 'A', 'C')
('B', 'C', 'A')
('C', 'A', 'B')
('C', 'B', 'A')

Permutations of A,B,C taken 2 at a time:
('A', 'B')
('A', 'C')
('B', 'A')
('B', 'C')
('C', 'A')
('C', 'B')

snippets.title: Listing Combinations with itertools
snippets.description: Use itertools.combinations for unordered selections
snippets.code:
from itertools import combinations

# Combinations of 3 things taken 2 at a time
C32 = combinations(['A', 'B', 'C'], 2)
print("Combinations of A,B,C taken 2 at a time:")
for i in list(C32):
    print(i)
snippets.expected_output:
Combinations of A,B,C taken 2 at a time:
('A', 'B')
('A', 'C')
('B', 'C')

snippets.title: Committee Assignment Problem
snippets.description: Apply combinations to solve committee assignment problem
snippets.code:
import math

# Assign 9 colleagues into 3 committees of 3 each

# First committee: select 3 out of 9
n1 = math.factorial(9) / (math.factorial(6) * math.factorial(3))

# Second committee: select 3 out of 6
n2 = math.factorial(6) / (math.factorial(3) * math.factorial(3))

# Third committee: select 3 out of 3
n3 = math.factorial(3) / (math.factorial(0) * math.factorial(3))

answer = n1 * n2 * n3
print(f"Answer: {n1:.0f} × {n2:.0f} × {n3:.0f} = {answer:.0f}")

snippets.title: Basic Probability Calculation
snippets.description: Calculate probability as favorable outcomes / total outcomes
snippets.code:
# Probability of 2 girls in 3 children

# Total possible cases (2^3 = 8)
Ns = 2**3

# Favorable outcomes: GGB, GBG, BGG
Na = 3

prob = Na / Ns
print(f"P(2 girls in 3 children) = {Na}/{Ns} = {prob}")

snippets.title: Probability Using Combination Formula
snippets.description: Calculate probability using combinatorics
snippets.code:
import math

# P(2 girls in 5 children)
# C(5,2): ways two girls can occur
Na = math.factorial(5) / (math.factorial(2) * math.factorial(3))

# Total outcomes
Ns = 2**5

prob = Na / Ns
print(f"Na = {Na:.0f}")
print(f"Ns = {Ns}")
print(f"Probability = {prob}")

snippets.title: Exercise A: Specific Sequence Probability
snippets.description: Calculate probability of specific sequence GGBBB using mathematics and enumeration

snippets.title: Manual Binomial Probability
snippets.description: Calculate binomial probabilities using the formula
snippets.code:
import math

# P(X=k) = C(n,k) * p^k * (1-p)^(n-k)
n = 2
p = 0.5

for k in [0, 1, 2]:
    coef = math.factorial(n) / (math.factorial(n-k) * math.factorial(k))
    prob = coef * (p**k) * ((1-p)**(n-k))
    print(f"P(k={k}) = {prob}")

snippets.title: Using scipy.stats.binom.pmf
snippets.description: Calculate binomial probabilities using scipy
snippets.code:
from scipy.stats import binom

# Parameters
p = 0.5
n = 2
success = [0, 1, 2]

# Create arrays for results
karray = []
probarray = []

for ki in success:
    karray.append(ki)
    probk = binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p)
    probarray.append(probk)
    print(f"k={ki}: P = {probk}")

snippets.title: Binomial Distribution Bar Plot
snippets.description: Visualize binomial distribution with bar chart
snippets.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Binomial distribution for 3 children (girls)
girls = [0, 1, 2, 3]
probs = [0.125, 0.375, 0.375, 0.125]

plt.bar(x=girls, height=probs, color="orange")
plt.xlabel('Number of Girls')
plt.ylabel('Probability')
plt.title('Probability of Girls in Family of 3 Children')
plt.show()

snippets.title: Complete Binomial Distribution Loop
snippets.description: Calculate full binomial distribution for n children
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import binom

# Parameters
n = 8
p = 0.5

# All possible success values
success = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probarray = [0] * (n + 1)

for ki in success:
    probk = binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p)
    probarray[ki] = probk
    print(f"k={ki}: P = {probk:.6f}")

snippets.title: Exercise B: Infection Analysis (p=0.45)
snippets.description: Calculate binomial probabilities for infection scenario with 25 contacts
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import binom

# Infection scenario parameters
p = 0.45
n = 25

success = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probarray = [0] * (n + 1)

print(f"Infection probabilities (n={n}, p={p}):")
for ki in success:
    probk = binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p)
    probarray[ki] = probk
    print(f"k={ki:2d}: P = {probk:.6f}")

parray1 = probarray  # Store for comparison

snippets.code:

# Bar plot of infection probabilities
plt.bar(x=success, height=probarray, color="blue")
plt.xlabel('Number of Infections (k)')

snippets.title: Exercise B: Infection Analysis (p=0.60)
snippets.description: Repeat infection analysis with higher transmission probability
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import binom
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Higher infection probability
p = 0.60
n = 25

success = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probarray = [binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p) for ki in success]

print(f"Infection probabilities (n={n}, p={p}):")
for ki in success:
    print(f"k={ki:2d}: P = {probarray[ki]:.6f}")

# Bar plot
plt.bar(x=success, height=probarray, color="red")
plt.xlabel('Number of Infections (k)')
plt.ylabel('Probability')
plt.title(f'Binomial Distribution (n={n}, p={p})')
plt.show()

snippets.title: Comparing Distributions Side-by-Side
snippets.description: Visual comparison of infection distributions with different p values
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import binom

n = 25
p_values = [0.45, 0.60]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

for idx, p in enumerate(p_values):
    success = np.arange(0, n + 1)
    probarray = [binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p) for ki in success]
    
    axes[idx].bar(success, probarray, color=['blue', 'red'][idx])
    axes[idx].set_xlabel('Number of Infections (k)')
    axes[idx].set_ylabel('Probability')
    axes[idx].set_title(f'Binomial (n={n}, p={p})')
    
    # Mark expected value
    expected = n * p
    axes[idx].axvline(x=expected, color='black', linestyle='--', label=f'E[X]={expected:.1f}')
    axes[idx].legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

snippets.title: Distribution Observations Summary
snippets.description: Calculate summary statistics for distribution comparison
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import binom

n = 25

for p in [0.45, 0.60]:
    expected = n * p
    variance = n * p * (1 - p)
    std_dev = np.sqrt(variance)
    
    # Find mode
    probs = [binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p) for ki in range(n + 1)]
    mode = np.argmax(probs)
    
    print(f"\np = {p}:")
    print(f"  Expected: E[X] = {expected:.1f}")
    print(f"  Std Dev: σ = {std_dev:.2f}")
    print(f"  Mode: k = {mode}")
    print(f"  P(k={mode}) = {probs[mode]:.4f}")
snippets.expected_output:
p = 0.45:
  Expected: E[X] = 11.2
  Std Dev: σ = 2.49
  Mode: k = 11
  P(k=11) = 0.1602

p = 0.60:
  Expected: E[X] = 15.0
  Std Dev: σ = 2.45
  Mode: k = 15
  P(k=15) = 0.1612

snippets.title: Q32 Connection: Sample Space
snippets.description: Verify sample space size for coin toss experiment
snippets.code:
from scipy.stats import binom

# Q32: Fair coin tossed 5 times
n_tosses = 5
sample_space_size = 2**n_tosses
print(f"Sample space size: |S| = {sample_space_size}")

# P(exactly 3 heads)
prob_3_heads = binom.pmf(k=3, n=5, p=0.5)
print(f"P(exactly 3 heads) = {prob_3_heads:.4f}")
print(f"Is this 0.60? {abs(prob_3_heads - 0.60) < 0.001}")
snippets.expected_output:
Sample space size: |S| = 32
P(exactly 3 heads) = 0.3125
Is this 0.60? False

snippets.title: Q35 Connection: Hypothesis Testing
snippets.description: Calculate p-value for testing infection rate hypothesis
snippets.code:
from scipy.stats import binom

# Q35: 9 of 11 contacts infected
# H0: p = 0.5, H1: p > 0.5
n = 11
observed_k = 9
p_null = 0.5

# P-value = P(X >= 9 | p = 0.5)
p_value = sum(binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p_null) for ki in range(9, n + 1))

print(f"P-value = P(X >= {observed_k} | n={n}, p={p_null})")
print(f"P-value = {p_value:.6f}")
print(f"Reject H0 at α=0.05? {p_value < 0.05}")
snippets.expected_output:
P-value = P(X >= 9 | n=11, p=0.5)
P-value = 0.032715
Reject H0 at α=0.05? True

snippets.title: Complete Lab Template
snippets.description: Complete template for lab submission
snippets.code:
"""
Week 9 Lab: Probability and Binomial Distribution
"""

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import math
from itertools import permutations, combinations
from scipy.stats import binom

# PART 1: Factorials
print(f"12!/10! = {math.factorial(12) / math.factorial(10)}")
print(f"6!/(2!4!) = {math.factorial(6) / (math.factorial(2) * math.factorial(4))}")

# EXERCISE A: P(GGBBB)
print(f"\nExercise A: P(GGBBB) = {1 / (2**5)}")

# EXERCISE B: Infection Analysis
def infection_analysis(n, p):
    success = np.arange(0, n + 1)
    probarray = [binom.pmf(k=ki, n=n, p=p) for ki in success]
    return success, probarray

success, probs1 = infection_analysis(25, 0.45)
success, probs2 = infection_analysis(25, 0.60)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
axes[0].bar(success, probs1, color='blue')
axes[0].set_title('p=0.45')
axes[1].bar(success, probs2, color='red')
axes[1].set_title('p=0.60')
plt.show()

# EXERCISE C: Quiz answers
# Q1: ___
# Q2: ___
# Q3: ___
# Q4: ___
# Q5: ___
